label: Airtable
description: Read and write records in Airtable bases using the Airtable REST API.

form.title: Connect Airtable
form.subtitle: Paste your Personal Access Token to give Anton scoped access to your Airtable bases.

methods.label: Personal Access Token
methods.description: Paste a PAT from Airtable's Developer Hub. Quickest path — no OAuth app setup required. Scopes are chosen when you create the token.
methods.how_to:
## Generate an Airtable Personal Access Token

Airtable's older per-base API keys are deprecated. Personal Access Tokens (PATs) are the current supported auth method.

### Steps

1. Log in to [airtable.com](https://airtable.com) and click your avatar in the top-right corner.
2. Go to **Developer hub** (or visit [airtable.com/create/tokens](https://airtable.com/create/tokens) directly).
3. Click **Create token**.
4. Give the token a descriptive name like `Anton`.
5. Under **Scopes**, add at minimum:
   - `data.records:read` — to read records
   - `data.records:write` — to create/update/delete records
   - `schema.bases:read` — to list tables and fields
6. Under **Access**, choose which bases or workspaces the token can reach. Pick **All current and future bases** for full access, or restrict to specific bases.
7. Click **Create token**. The token is shown **once** — copy it immediately.
8. Paste it into the **Personal Access Token** field below.

### Notes

- Tokens start with `pat`. If yours doesn't, you may have copied an old API key instead.
- If you lose the token, delete it in Developer Hub and create a new one — Anton can accept a replacement without any other changes.
- Scopes can't be edited after creation; delete and recreate if you need to add scopes later.

fields.name: personal_access_token
fields.label: Personal Access Token
fields.description: Starts with 'pat'. Found in airtable.com → avatar → Developer hub → Personal access tokens.

fields.name: base_id
fields.label: Base ID (optional)
fields.description: Restrict Anton to a specific base. Leave blank to access all bases the token can reach. Found in the base URL: airtable.com/appXXXX/...

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (bring your own client)
methods.description: Authorize via Airtable's OAuth flow using your own OAuth client credentials. More setup required but tokens are user-scoped and revocable from Airtable's connected apps page.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via OAuth 2.0

Use this method if you want a browser-based consent flow rather than managing a token manually. You must register your own OAuth client in Airtable's Developer Hub.

### Steps

1. Go to [airtable.com/create/oauth](https://airtable.com/create/oauth) and click **Register new OAuth integration**.
2. Fill in a name (e.g. `Anton`) and set the redirect URI to `http://localhost` (Anton uses a local server to capture the code).
3. Under **Scopes**, enable at minimum: `data.records:read`, `data.records:write`, `schema.bases:read`.
4. Save the integration and copy the **Client ID** and **Client secret**.
5. Paste both into the fields below and click **Submit**.
6. A browser window will open to Airtable's consent screen — approve the requested permissions.
7. Anton exchanges the authorization code for a refresh token and stores it locally.

### Notes

- Airtable requires PKCE for OAuth clients. Anton handles this automatically.
- To revoke access, visit **airtable.com → avatar → Connected apps** and remove the entry.
- If the consent screen shows a warning that the app is unverified, that's expected for self-registered integrations.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From Airtable Developer Hub → your OAuth integration → Client ID.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID. Shown once when you create the integration — store it safely.
